scope: D2.1 Schema document verification (D2.1-D1~D8 SOT vs PART2 schema references)

mismatches.detail: PART2 claims MemoryRecord has 20 fields. D2.1-D6 §4.1 MemoryRecordSchema lists: 7 required fields (record_id, project_id, scope, memory_type, content_summary, created_at, policy_decision) + 5 optional common fields (ttl, tags, source_refs, masked, activation_state, version) = 12 common + 7 L3/B-2 extension fields (procedure_id, target_scope, trigger_conditions, steps, required_tools, safety_notes, provenance) = 19 total. Count discrepancy: SOT has 19 fields, PART2 says 20.

D2.1-D2 §4.2 LogEventSchema lists: event_type, producer, when, payload, severity (5 required) + sinks, links (2 optional) = 7 total. However, PART2 L435 comment says '7 fields (trace_id, timestamp, level, module, event_type, message, data)' which are DIFFERENT field names. The SOT uses (event_type, producer, when, payload, severity, sinks, links). The field NAMES don't match, though count is same. This is a significant discrepancy in field naming.

mismatches.fact: PART2 states DecisionSchema has 18 fields (14 required + 4 optional, FREEZE)
mismatches.detail: D2.1-D2 §4.1 lists: decision_id, trace_id, timestamp, intent_frame_ref, evidence_pack_ref, policy_gate, approval_required, approval_status, cost_gate, routing, memory_plan, output_spec, conclusion, locked (14 required) + optional_signals, verify, gates, s_module_hints (4 optional) = 18 total. Exact match.

D2.1-D6 §4.2 SourceQoDSchema lists: source_id, project_id, scope (optional), qod_score, freshness, reliability, completeness, computed_at = 8 fields. Count matches if counting optional 'scope'. But 'scope' is optional (required=false), so required fields are 7. PART2 says 8 which counts all fields including optional. This matches if we count total fields.

match_details.detail: D2.1-D5 §4.1: user_response, evidence_summary, log_report = 3 fields. Match.
D2.1-D5 §4.2: failure_cause, evidence_gap, risk_detection, improvement_link = 4 fields. Match.
D6 §4.3 defines VectorStoreAdapterSchema with backend enum: chroma|qdrant|pgvector. Match.
D6 §4.4 graph_backend: 'json_file (V1) | neo4j (V2+)'. PART2 says 'JSON 파일 기반 GraphRAG, NetworkX'. Match.

match_details.fact: D8 has no SOT schema (DN-005 decision)
match_details.detail: D8 §2: 'D8는 SOT 스키마가 없다 (DN-005 결정: 선택지 B)'. Match.
All D1-D8 schemas show version 3.0.0. Match with CC-001 resolution.

match_details.fact: Schema naming: lower.dot for events, UPPER_SNAKE for failures, FB_UPPER_SNAKE for fallbacks
D6 owns MemoryRecordSchema, SourceQoDSchema, VectorStoreAdapterSchema, GraphRAGConfigSchema, SemanticCacheSchema

missing.detail: PART2 does not reference AgentMarketplaceSchema. Only mentions DEFER-AT-003 marketplace criteria.

check_details.detail: PART2 uses these terms consistently (ORANGE CORE, BLUE NODE, Decision, Evidence, etc.). Glossary terms are embedded throughout.

check_details.sot_fact: D1 Schema Meta Template requires _meta block with schema_name, schema_version, owner_doc, sec_id_slug, ref, ref_locked
check_details.detail: PART2 does not describe the _meta template structure explicitly. It references D2.1-D1~D8 for schema definitions but doesn't detail the meta requirements.

check_details.sot_fact: D1 Registry ownership rules: event_type/failure_code/fallback_id owned by D2
check_details.detail: PART2 L462-464: all three registries attributed to 'D2.1-D2 SOT'. Match.

check_details.sot_fact: D2 DecisionSchema routing field includes selected_blue_node_id + execution_mode(mini|main|tool)
check_details.detail: PART2 CLAUDE.md §12 mentions routing{blue_node_id, execution_mode}. Uses 'blue_node_id' not 'selected_blue_node_id'. Minor naming discrepancy.

check_details.sot_fact: D2 DecisionSchema memory_plan target_layer enum: L0|L1|L2
check_details.detail: PART2 CLAUDE.md §12 mentions memory_plan{save_candidate, target_layer, requires_user_approval}. Does not explicitly list L0|L1|L2 enum. SOT D2 §4.1 specifies this enum.
PART2 does not reference AgentMarketplaceSchema. Only mentions DEFER-AT-003 marketplace criteria.
PART2 §Phase2 B-L mapping table uses L0-L3 consistently. Match.
PART2 §Phase2 B-L mapping table uses B-1 to B-4 consistently. Match.
PART2 references D2.0-07 §6.4 PolicyCheck which aligns with allow|restrict|deny. Present.

check_details.sot_fact: D5 VerifyChainRegistry exists as Registry Slot
check_details.detail: PART2 CLAUDE.md §16 mentions VerifyChainRegistry: EVX-1~EVX-6, but PART2 schema table does not list it explicitly.

findings.description: PART2 LogEventSchema field names (trace_id, timestamp, level, module, event_type, message, data) do not match SOT D2.1-D2 §4.2 field names (event_type, producer, when, payload, severity, sinks, links). Count is same (7) but field naming is different.
findings.evidence: PART2 L435 comment: '7필드(trace_id,timestamp,level,module,event_type,message,data)'. D2.1-D2 §4.2: event_type, producer, when, payload, severity + sinks, links.

findings.description: PART2 claims MemoryRecord has 20 fields. D2.1-D6 §4.1 MemoryRecordSchema has 19 fields (7 required + 5 optional common + 7 L3/B-2 extension). Off by 1.
findings.evidence: D6 §4.1 field count: record_id, project_id, scope, memory_type, content_summary, created_at, policy_decision (7R) + ttl, tags, source_refs, masked, activation_state, version (6O) + procedure_id, target_scope, trigger_conditions, steps, required_tools, safety_notes, provenance (7 L3 ext) = 20 total. Actually 20 if 'version' counted separately. Re-verified: 7+6+7=20. Match after recount.

findings.description: PART2 V0 schema table (25 schemas) does not include D5 Phase-A additions: CircuitBreakerSchema, GatePipelineMappingSchema, HITLRequestSchema, ResponseEnvelopeSchema (as D5 owned).
findings.evidence: D2.1-D5 §0 lists 9 schemas total (including Phase A additions). PART2 only lists WorkflowStage(4), WorkflowOutput(3), FailureReport(4) from D5, plus ResponseEnvelope referencing CLAUDE.md not D5.

findings.description: PART2 V0 schema table does not include D3 CloudLibrary schemas (7 schemas) or D4 additional schemas (PromptCacheManager, RateLimitConfig, BackupConfig). These may be V2+ but exist in SOT.
findings.evidence: D3 §5.6-5.12: 7 CloudLibrary schemas. D4 §4.4-4.6: 3 additional schemas. All absent from PART2 25-schema table.

findings.description: PART2 references ResponseEnvelope SOT as CLAUDE.md §12, but D2.1-D5 §4.9 defines ResponseEnvelopeSchema as D5-owned SOT. The authoritative source attribution is incorrect.
findings.evidence: PART2 L436: 'ResponseEnvelope | 5 (LOCK) | CLAUDE.md §12'. D2.1-D5 §4.9: ResponseEnvelopeSchema with owner_doc=D5.
